name: Retune Hot-Band CTA Traversal On The 128x128 PTX Grid

summary.name: Retune Hot-Band CTA Traversal On The 128x128 PTX Grid
summary.hypothesis: Round 3/20 recovered from the 256x128 loss and set a new best at 24.177664 ms, but the hot-band PTX kernel's NCU signature barely moved relative to the previous PTX base: kernel time remained about 32.687 ms and register count stayed at 200/thread. That suggests the unroll tweak was only a small perturbation rather than a structural feed breakthrough. The best next move is therefore a cheap orthogonal traversal/locality test on the proven PTX base. The current branch still launches a 60x50 hot-band grid while only two 4-warp CTAs can stay resident per SM, so a grouped-row or serpentine traversal remap is the best low-risk probe of inter-CTA locality.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Inter-CTA locality and traversal efficiency on the current 128x128 PTX hot-band grid under low occupancy.
summary.risk: Low to moderate. The surface is small and reversible, but previous CTA-order experiments have had mixed evidence, so the upside is bounded.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime versus the 24.177664 ms base
ranking_notes: This is the cheapest orthogonal family still open on top of the current best PTX base.
It is ranked ahead of another PTX control tweak because the latest exploit win did not materially change hot-band counters.
If this stays flat, the search can return to PTX-local tuning with little regret.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if one traversal tweak leaves hot-band time and cache-facing counters flat after a single measured round.

notes: Node C may now implement this one candidate.
